Machine Learning and Statistics Terms

This A-to-Z glossary compiles terminology used across the notebooks in this project, with professional definitions and expanded explanations.

  • Total terms: 183

Terms

A

ACE

ACE is a technical acronym used in statistics or machine learning. This term is part of the core vocabulary used for statistical modeling, machine learning, and causal analysis. In applied analytics, it is used to improve model validity, interpretability, and decision quality. Acronym: ACE Acronym Definition: Acronym used in the notebooks; expansion should be confirmed from original source context.

Source notebooks: inst/notebooks/Time_series/structural-causal-models- (SCMs)-r.qmd

ADF

ADF is an augmented dickey-fuller test. A statistical unit-root test used to assess stationarity in time-series processes. In applied analytics, it is used to improve model validity, interpretability, and decision quality. Acronym: ADF Acronym Definition: Augmented Dickey-Fuller

Source notebooks: inst/notebooks/05-deep-causal-learners/05-04-01-causal-time-series-granger-causality-var-r.qmd

AIPW

AIPW is an augmented inverse probability weighting estimator. A semi-parametric doubly robust estimator that combines outcome regression and propensity weighting to estimate causal effects consistently if either nuisance model is correctly specified. In causal inference practice, this concept helps define estimands, identification assumptions, or robust estimation procedures. Acronym: AIPW Acronym Definition: Augmented Inverse Probability Weighting

Source notebooks: inst/notebooks/01-tree-based-Models/01-08-multi-arm-causal-boost-r.qmd

AISTATS

AISTATS is a technical acronym used in statistics or machine learning. This term is part of the core vocabulary used for statistical modeling, machine learning, and causal analysis. In applied analytics, it is used to improve model validity, interpretability, and decision quality. Acronym: AISTATS Acronym Definition: International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Statistics

Source notebooks: inst/notebooks/05-deep-causal-learners/05-02-00-DeepCausalML-generative-latent-variable-causal-models-introduction-r.qmd

ANM

ANM is a technical acronym used in statistics or machine learning. This term is part of the core vocabulary used for statistical modeling, machine learning, and causal analysis. In applied analytics, it is used to improve model validity, interpretability, and decision quality. Acronym: ANM Acronym Definition: Additive Noise Model

Source notebooks: inst/notebooks/05-deep-causal-learners/05-03-03-DeepCausalML-gradient-based-neural-learning-GranDag-r.qmd

ATE (Average Treatment Effect)

ATE (Average Treatment Effect) is an average treatment effect. The expected difference in outcomes between treatment and control across the full target population. In causal inference practice, this concept helps define estimands, identification assumptions, or robust estimation procedures. Acronym: ATE Acronym Definition: Average Treatment Effect

Source notebooks: inst/notebooks/01-tree-based-Models/01-00-casual-tree-introduction-r.qmd

ATTE

ATTE is an average treatment effect on the treated. The expected treatment effect for units that actually received treatment. In causal inference practice, this concept helps define estimands, identification assumptions, or robust estimation procedures. Acronym: ATTE Acronym Definition: Average Treatment Effect on the Treated

Source notebooks: inst/notebooks/04-double-debiased-learners/04-06-double-ml-weighted-double-ml.qmd

AUTOC

AUTOC is a technical acronym used in statistics or machine learning. This term is part of the core vocabulary used for statistical modeling, machine learning, and causal analysis. In applied analytics, it is used to improve model validity, interpretability, and decision quality. Acronym: AUTOC Acronym Definition: Area Under the Targeting/Ordering Curve

Source notebooks: inst/notebooks/01-tree-based-Models/01-02-causal-forest-r.qmd

B

Baseline Arm

Baseline Arm is a statistical or machine learning concept. This term is part of the core vocabulary used for statistical modeling, machine learning, and causal analysis. In applied analytics, it is used to improve model validity, interpretability, and decision quality. Acronym: BA Acronym Definition: Acronym used in the notebooks; expansion should be confirmed from original source context.

BGM

BGM is a technical acronym used in statistics or machine learning. This term is part of the core vocabulary used for statistical modeling, machine learning, and causal analysis. In applied analytics, it is used to improve model validity, interpretability, and decision quality. Acronym: BGM Acronym Definition: Bijective Generation Mechanisms

Source notebooks: inst/notebooks/05-deep-causal-learners/05-02-06-DeepCausalML-deep-structural-causal-model-DSCM-r.qmd

Boosting

Boosting is a boosting ensemble methodology. A stage-wise ensemble strategy that sequentially fits weak learners to residual structure to improve predictive performance. In machine learning practice, it is typically applied to capture nonlinear structure, interactions, and out-of-sample generalization behavior. Acronym: N/A Acronym Definition: Not applicable (no standard acronym for this term).

Source notebooks: inst/notebooks/01-tree-based-Models/01-06-causal-xgboost-r.qmd

C

CART

CART is a classification and regression tree methodology. A recursive partitioning approach that learns piecewise prediction rules and serves as a building block for many tree-based learners. In applied analytics, it is used to improve model validity, interpretability, and decision quality. Acronym: CART Acronym Definition: Classification and Regression Trees

Source notebooks: inst/notebooks/01-tree-based-Models/01-00-casual-tree-introduction-r.qmd

CASTLE

CASTLE is a causal structure learning concept. This term is used to represent or recover directed dependency structure for intervention analysis and counterfactual reasoning. In structure learning workflows, it supports reasoning about directional dependencies, interventions, and counterfactual queries. Acronym: CASTLE Acronym Definition: Causal Structure Learning via Regularization

Source notebooks: inst/notebooks/05-deep-causal-learners/05-03-05-DeepCausalML-causal-structural-learning-regularization-CASTLE-r.qmd

CASTLE — CAusal STructure LEarning Regularization

CASTLE — CAusal STructure LEarning Regularization is a causal structure learning concept. This term is used to represent or recover directed dependency structure for intervention analysis and counterfactual reasoning. In causal inference practice, this concept helps define estimands, identification assumptions, or robust estimation procedures. Acronym: CCSLR Acronym Definition: Acronym used in the notebooks; expansion should be confirmed from original source context.

CATE

CATE is a conditional average treatment effect. The expected treatment effect conditional on covariates, used to characterize effect heterogeneity across subpopulations. In causal inference practice, this concept helps define estimands, identification assumptions, or robust estimation procedures. Acronym: CATE Acronym Definition: Conditional Average Treatment Effect

Source notebooks: inst/notebooks/01-tree-based-Models/01-03-causal-survival-forest-r.qmd, inst/notebooks/01-tree-based-Models/01-06-causal-xgboost-r.qmd

Deep Causal Learning

DML

DML is a double machine learning framework. An orthogonalized estimation framework that leverages machine learning for nuisance components while retaining valid statistical inference for target causal parameters. In causal inference practice, this concept helps define estimands, identification assumptions, or robust estimation procedures. Acronym: DML Acronym Definition: Double Machine Learning

Double Machine Learning with Instrumental Variables

This term is used to represent or recover directed dependency structure for intervention analysis and counterfactual reasoning. In structure learning workflows, it supports reasoning about directional dependencies, interventions, and counterfactual queries. Acronym: DML Acronym Definition: Double Machine Learning

IV

IV is an instrumental variable framework. An identification strategy for endogenous treatment settings that uses exogenous variation from valid instruments to recover causal effects. In causal inference practice, this concept helps define estimands, identification assumptions, or robust estimation procedures. Acronym: IV Acronym Definition: Instrumental Variable

TMLE

TMLE is a targeted maximum likelihood estimation. A doubly robust estimation procedure that updates initial predictions to optimize bias-variance tradeoffs while supporting asymptotically efficient inference. In causal inference practice, this concept helps define estimands, identification assumptions, or robust estimation procedures. Acronym: TMLE Acronym Definition: Targeted Maximum Likelihood Estimation

V

VAE

VAE is a variational autoencoder. A latent-variable generative model trained with variational inference to learn probabilistic representations of complex data. In deep learning pipelines, it is often used for representation learning, high-dimensional prediction, or generative modeling. Acronym: VAE Acronym Definition: Variational Autoencoder

VAR

VAR is a causal structure learning concept. This term is used to represent or recover directed dependency structure for intervention analysis and counterfactual reasoning. In structure learning workflows, it supports reasoning about directional dependencies, interventions, and counterfactual queries. Acronym: VAR Acronym Definition: Vector Autoregression

Source notebooks: inst/notebooks/05-deep-causal-learners/05-04-01-causal-time-series-granger-causality-var-r.qmd
